title: Discrete divergence boundedness for incompressible cylinder-flow outputs
transformation_preconditions: The output velocity field and mesh connectivity are available.
A concrete discrete divergence operator and boundary treatment are declared.
The tolerance is calibrated against numerical discretization error or trusted reference behavior.

boundary_condition_compatibility.reason: The current asset has not fixed the discrete operator, boundary weights, or tolerance calibration needed for a retained MR verdict.

output_mapping.description: Evaluate the divergence statistic on the predicted velocity field.

exclusion_rules: Defer verdicts when the discrete divergence operator is not fixed.
Do not classify a high divergence value as a model fault without calibrated tolerance.
Treat this as a diagnostic candidate until boundary treatment is specified.

verdict_interpretation.skip: Required operator, boundary treatment, or runtime artifact is unavailable.
verdict_interpretation.out-of-relation-domain: The source case is outside the incompressible-flow assumptions represented by the card.
verdict_interpretation.inconclusive: The diagnostic value cannot be interpreted as a retained MR verdict.

claim_limitations: This is a deferred candidate card. Divergence is treated as an incompressible continuity or mass-conservation constraint, not as a Noether-derived conservation law, and no SUT verdict is claimed.
